sections.paragraph:
ἐπέκεινα (=ἐπʼ ἐκεῖνα) adv. at a more advanced point, farther on, beyond (Hdt. et al.; LXX w. and without gen.; En, Just.; Tat. 20, 2; temporal Just., D. 88, 1; Ath., R. 68, 16) οἱ ἐ. ἀδελφοί the brothers farther on MPol 20:1 (Appian, Hann. 6 §21 τοῖς ἐπέκεινα Κέλτοις; Herodian 4, 15, 3 οἱ ἐ. βάρβαροι). W. gen. (En 24:2; Chares [after 323 b.c.] : 125 Fgm. 5 Jac. [in Athen. 13, 35, 575b]; Arrian, Anab. 5, 5, 3 ἐπ. Καυκάσου; Maximus Tyr. 11, 10e τούτων ἐπ. ἐλθεῖν; Synes., Ep. 148 p. 285b ἐ. Θούλης) Βαβυλῶνος beyond Babylon Ac 7:43 (Am 5:27).—Sv.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

gen. gen. = genitive

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

Maximus Tyr Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

Synes Synes , IV–V a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 361
